responses.response: This is the strongest challenge to our S3 position because it attacks not our conclusion but our reasoning: we set pre-implementation conditions (teacher retraining, non-state intake agreement, constitutional embedding, county pilot) as though structure and its supporting components were separable levers each verifiable in isolation. A8 argues the equity effect is a confounded bundle — Finland's outcomes emerged from structure plus teacher policy plus early support jointly, and single-factor attribution is genuinely hard in the comparative literature. If the components are causally inseparable, then our staged-condition framing is itself an evidential error: retraining '80% of upper-grade staff before the first cohort' treats teacher policy as a precondition to structure rather than as a co-constitutive part of the same intervention, and a single-county pilot cannot isolate the structural lever if the effect only appears when all components co-occur over 10-15 years. We accept this on the merits. Our position that S3 requires credible mitigation of binding constraints stands, but the mitigations should be reframed as integral design components implemented together, not as sequential gates, and the pilot must be evaluated as a bundle-fidelity test (did all components deploy?) rather than a structural-lever test. This tightens rather than loosens our evidential demand, so we maintain the oppose-without-mitigation stance while correcting the separability assumption.
responses.new_condition: We move to conditional support only if S3 is implemented as an inseparable bundle — delayed selection plus completed teacher stabilisation/retraining plus early-support infrastructure plus non-state intake restraint plus constitutional embedding deployed together in a single-county pilot evaluated for full bundle-fidelity (all components present) over a minimum horizon long enough for peer effects to emerge, with published longitudinal data before national scaling. We drop the sequential-gate framing (retraining 'before' the first cohort) as evidentially unsound, since the components are not separable levers; partial-bundle implementation remains a stance we oppose, because copying only the cheap structural change is precisely the failure mode A8 identifies.
